bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede, so I am targeting maximum utility by holding my strongest values across all issues. The opponent's initial offer is the lowest-utility bid for me on every issue, so I read it as an opening anchor rather than a meaningful concession signal.

The opponent improved from a utility of about 0.20 to 0.44 for me, so they are conceding but still far from a strong agreement. Since time is only 0.02, I should stay near my maximum utility while avoiding an exact repeat of my prior all-valueA bid; I concede only on a low-weight issueE, targeting about 0.98 utility.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.96 because it is still very early at t=0.04, so there is little reason to make large concessions yet. The opponent has improved their offers from very low utility to me up to about 0.48, showing some concession, but still far from an acceptable split. I will concede only on lower-weight issuesD and issueE while holding firm on the high-weight issuesA, issueB, and issueC.

I am targeting a very high utility bid around 0.94 because it is still very early at t=0.06 and there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent improved from their opening offer but then worsened on issueA and issueB at t=0.06, so their behavior does not show steady concession; I will hold firm on high-weight issueA, issueB, and issueC while conceding slightly on low-weight issueE.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.92 because it is still very early in the negotiation, so there is little reason to concede on the high-weight issues A and B. The opponent initially improved up to t=0.04 but then moved backward to lower-utility offers for me, so I read them as not reliably conceding; I will only concede further on low-weight issues D and E for now.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.90 because it is still very early at t=0.10, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s offers improved briefly for me but then moved back down, so I do not see reliable concession; I will hold firm on high-weight issueA and issueB while conceding only a low-weight issue to avoid repeating my last bid.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for me while making concessions mainly on the lowest-weight issues. The opponent’s latest valid offer gives me about 0.48 utility and shows some movement on issueA/issueB/issueC, but they continue pushing issueD and issueE to valueE, so I will concede on those lower-importance issues while holding issueA, issueB, and issueC at valueA.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.86 because it is still early at t=0.14, while making a small concession on a low-weight issue to keep movement toward agreement. The opponent’s valid offers have fluctuated rather than steadily improved for me, mostly staying low utility and demanding worse values on issueD/issueE, so I will concede issueE while holding firm on the high-weight issues issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.86 because it is still early at t=0.16, so there is no need to make major concessions on the highly weighted issues A, B, and C. The opponent’s offers remain low for me and fluctuate rather than steadily improve, but they consistently put issueD and issueE at valueE, so I concede mainly on those low-weight issues while avoiding an exact repeat of my previous bid.

I am targeting a high utility around 0.84 because it is still early at t=0.18 and the opponent’s offers remain low for me, with their latest bid worth only about 0.42. Their history consistently sets issueD and issueE to valueE, so I concede those low-weight issues while holding firm on my highest-weight issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.80 because it is early at t=0.20, so there is no need to concede heavily on the high-weight issues A and B. The opponent’s offers have improved only modestly and inconsistently for me, but they consistently push issueD and issueE to valueE, so I concede those low-weight issues while making a new concession on issueC to encourage movement.

At t=0.22 it is still early, so I am targeting a strong utility while making a modest additional concession to keep momentum. The opponent’s valid offers have mostly stayed around low-to-mid utility for me and consistently push issueD/issueE to valueE, so I concede those low-weight issues while holding firm on high-weight issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a utility around 0.74, which is a modest concession from my last offer while still protecting the high-weight issues A and B. At t=0.24 it is still relatively early, so I should not drop to the opponent’s low offers yet, but their repeated insistence on issueD and issueE as valueE suggests those are acceptable concessions for me. Their offers have improved somewhat for my utility but remain far below my recent bids, so I will concede slightly on issueB while keeping issueA strong.

I am targeting about 0.70 utility, keeping firm on the highest-weight issues while making a meaningful concession on issueC and fully conceding the low-weight issueD/issueE. At t=0.26 there is still time, so I should not drop close to the opponent’s current offers, but their recent bids repeatedly put issueC around valueC and issueD/issueE at valueE, so I am using those as lower-cost concessions.

At t=0.28 we are still early enough to target a strong utility around 0.68 while continuing gradual concessions. The opponent’s offers have not consistently improved for me and currently give only about 0.40 utility, but they consistently push issueD and issueE to valueE, so I keep conceding those low-weight issues while making a small tradeoff between issueB and issueC compared with my last bid.

At t=0.30 I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.64, conceding gradually from my last offer without dropping to the opponent’s much lower bids. The opponent’s valid offers have mostly stayed around valueC/valueD on high-weight issues and valueE on low-weight issues, so I keep issueA firm while conceding further on issueC and leaving issueD/issueE at valueE.

At t=0.32 I am still aiming for a moderate utility around 0.64 rather than making a large late-stage concession. The opponent’s valid offers remain low for me and fluctuate around valueC/valueD on the high-weight issues, so I am rotating concessions while holding reasonable value on issueA and issueB and keeping issueD/issueE fully conceded as low-weight tradeoffs.

I am targeting a moderate concession around utility 0.58, still above the opponent’s best offers to me but moving closer as we enter the mid-game at t=0.34. The opponent has mostly held around valueD/valueE with only limited concessions, so I will concede on issueB while protecting issueA, one of my highest-weight issues, and keep the low-weight issues already at valueE.

I am targeting about 0.58 utility: enough concession for t=0.36, but still above the opponent’s latest offer to me, which is only about 0.48. The opponent has repeatedly kept issueD and issueE at valueE and has recently offered issueA/issueB around valueC, so I concede the low-weight D/E fully and match issueA=valueC while holding issueB slightly higher because it is high-weight for me.

I am targeting about 0.56 utility: slightly above the opponent’s latest offer to me (~0.54) while still conceding from my earlier bids. At t=0.38 there is moderate time pressure but not enough to fully accept their exact position; their recent move to issueB=valueB suggests some concession, while they remain firm on issueD/issueE=valueE, so I only ask a small improvement on low-weight issueD.

I targeted a moderate utility around 0.58, slightly above the opponent’s latest offer to me while still making concessions on low-weight issues D and E. At t=0.40 there is still time, so I should not fully capitulate, but the opponent has clearly moved toward issueA=valueC and issueB=valueB, so I keep those and ask only for a small improvement on issueC.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.54, conceding further from my last offer while preserving some value on the higher-weight issueB. The opponent’s recent bids cluster around issueA=valueC, issueC=valueD, issueD/E=valueE, and they previously offered issueB=valueB at t=0.38, so I am proposing that signaled acceptable package rather than repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.58, holding the higher-weight issues A and B near the opponent’s best prior concessions while keeping low-weight issues D and E fully conceded. The opponent has shown some willingness to offer issueA=valueC and issueB=valueB, but their latest bid regressed to issueB=valueC and issueC=valueD, so I will make a small firm counter rather than drop to their last offer yet.

I am targeting a utility around 0.54, conceding from my last 0.58 bid because we are approaching the midpoint but not yet at the deadline. The opponent has repeatedly stayed around offers worth 0.48 to me, with one prior offer matching this bid, so this keeps my high-weight issueB at valueB while conceding lower/medium issues they seem to demand. No opponent messages were provided, so I rely only on the offer pattern.

I targeted a utility of about 0.52, making a modest mid-game concession from my prior offer while still staying above the opponent’s latest offer utility to me. The opponent has mostly held issueD and issueE at valueE and recently settled around issueA/issueB as valueC, so I concede on those low-utility and signaled areas while asking for issueC: valueC instead of valueD.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.54, which is slightly above the opponent’s current repeated offer of 0.48 to me while still reflecting mid-game concessions. At t=0.50, I should not insist on my earlier high-utility bids, and the opponent has previously offered issueA=valueC, issueB=valueB, issueC=valueD, issueD=valueE, issueE=valueE, so this bid uses an option they have already signaled may be acceptable. There are no opponent messages to weigh beyond the offer pattern.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.52, conceding substantially on the low-weight issueD and issueE while still asking for a small improvement on issueC. The opponent has been holding at issueA/valueC, issueB/valueC, issueC/valueD, issueD/valueE, issueE/valueE, but they previously offered issueC/valueC, so I will test that tradeoff without repeating my immediately previous bid.

I am targeting about 0.54 utility by holding a better value on high-weight issueB while accepting concessions on lower-utility issueC/D/E. At t=0.54 we are in the mid-game, and the opponent has mostly stabilized around issueA:valueC, issueB:valueC, issueC:valueD, issueD:valueE, issueE:valueE, but they previously offered issueB:valueB at t=0.38, so this bid uses a concession pattern they have already signaled may be possible.

I am targeting a moderate mid-game utility around 0.52, conceding further on the high-weight A/B issues only to valueC while keeping a small gain on issueC. The opponent has mostly offered variants around issueA/issueB = valueC and issueC = valueD with issueD/E fixed at valueE, though their latest offer regressed, so I will not chase that regression but will make a plausible compromise. With no messages to interpret, I rely on the offer pattern and avoid exactly repeating my prior bid.

I am targeting about 0.54 utility by keeping issueB at valueB while accepting lower values on issueC through issueE. At t=0.58 there is moderate time pressure, but not enough to drop below bids the opponent has already signaled, and the opponent previously offered this exact valid combination around t=0.38, suggesting it may be acceptable.
